issues: The draft describes this as a 'Centennial Cup playoff game' — the OCR indicates this is a B.C.-Alberta Centennial Cup hockey playoff, which corresponds to the Doyle Cup (BC vs. Alberta inter-league playoff), not the national Centennial Cup itself.
The draft omits scorers Brian Puhalsky, Tracy Georgsen, and Bob Duchak for Red Deer, listing only Dixon Ward and Richard Anderson.
The draft omits the penalty details: Red Deer outshot Richmond 46-34, and the game had 28 minor penalties, 20 majors, and 20 game misconducts — indicating a very rough, chippy game.
The draft omits context that before this series, the Sockeyes had gone undefeated in playoffs, including a 2-0 sweep of Quesnel Millionaires (PCJHL champions).
The headline is AI-generated and does not reflect an actual headline from the OCR (only a CP wire paragraph is present, no distinct headline text).
The series result noted in the draft (2-2) is correct, but the draft omits that Red Deer won the previous game 6-3 on Saturday.
review.correctedDescriptionShort: Red Deer Rustlers rout the Richmond Sockeyes 7-1 in a chippy B.C.–Alberta Centennial Cup inter-league playoff game, evening the best-of-seven series at 2-2.
review.correctedDescriptionLong: In a chippy B.C.–Alberta Centennial Cup inter-league playoff game in Red Deer on April 12, 1987, the Red Deer Rustlers thumped the Richmond Sockeyes 7-1, evening the best-of-seven series at 2-2. Dixon Ward and Richard Anderson each scored twice for the AJHL champion Rustlers, with Brian Puhalsky, Tracy Georgsen, and Bob Duchak also scoring; Red Deer led 3-0 after the first period and 6-0 after the second, and outshot Richmond 46-34. Dave Tomlinson scored the Sockeyes' lone goal. The game was marked by heavy penalties — 28 minors, 20 majors, and 20 game misconducts — and the CP wire item notes that prior to this series, the Sockeyes had gone undefeated in playoffs, including a 2-0 sweep of Quesnel Millionaires (PCJHL champions).
review.notes: No distinct headline is visible in the OCR — this is a CP wire dispatch. The corrected headline field has been left blank accordingly. The series described is the Doyle Cup (BC vs. Alberta inter-league), not the national Centennial Cup; the draft's labeling of this as a "Centennial Cup playoff" follows the OCR's own wording ("B.C.-Alberta Centennial Cup hockey playoff"), but editor should note this is the Doyle Cup stage of the path to the national Centennial Cup.
